tweets.text: .@SecBlinken: The protection and welfare of Americans abroad remains the State Department’s most vital and enduring mission. https://t.co/JAoFqJvT0R
.@SecBlinken: ​​More than 123,000 people have been safely flown out of Afghanistan. That includes about 6,000 American citizens. https://t.co/FvY5zTW36W

.@SecBlinken and @SecBecerra outline critical steps world leaders must take now to prepare for future pandemics. Transparency, accountability, and the modernization of global health systems will ensure the legacy of COVID-19 is a safer world for all. https://t.co/yR1OSlpDo7

.@DeptofDefense service members, diplomats, and others are supporting evacuees @NAS_Sigonella in Italy. Many U.S. government agencies are working around the clock to bring Americans home and help Afghans seeking refuge. https://t.co/gDQ9zuefaY

The U.S. strongly supports the creation of the UN Permanent Forum and today’s inaugural International Day for People of #AfricanDescent. Racial justice & equity are immediate priorities for the Administration’s foreign policy, including engagements at the @UN. https://t.co/Ww3d4PSJeb
.@SecBlinken: The main point I want to drive home here today is that America’s work in Afghanistan continues. We have a plan for what’s next, and we’re putting it into action. https://t.co/anrLzq94g2

.@SecBlinken: We will stay focused on counterterrorism. The Taliban seek international legitimacy and support. Our message is: any legitimacy and any support will have to be earned. https://t.co/8vbL2K6KEV

.@SecBlinken: We will continue our relentless efforts to help Americans, foreign nationals, and Afghans leave Afghanistan if they choose. Our commitment to them and to all Americans in Afghanistan – and everywhere in the world – continues. https://t.co/s2s46yvQ2C

.@SecBlinken: We have suspended our diplomatic presence in Kabul and transferred our operations to Doha, Qatar. For the time being, we will use this post in Doha to manage our diplomacy with Afghanistan. https://t.co/6flzBwV0bX

.@SecBlinken: More than 123,000 people have been safely flown out of Afghanistan. That includes about 6,000 American citizens. This has been a massive military, diplomatic, and humanitarian undertaking – one of the most difficult in our nation’s history. https://t.co/jOF9t6NFgI
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers remarks on our efforts in Afghanistan since August 14 and the way forward. https://t.co/MCHwXwlIRS

Today at approximately 5 p.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will deliver remarks on our efforts in Afghanistan since August 14 and the way forward. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz and https://t.co/IsfE2b9yku. https://t.co/bu7bjjbV6l

Today, on International Day of the Victims of Enforced Disappearances, we honor those who have been forcibly disappeared. @UnderSecStateJ: "All human beings are equal in rights and dignity, no matter where they live, who they are, what they say, or what they believe." https://t.co/OTvwKx7zU4

Sharing safe and effective vaccines with the world is a key part of defeating COVID-19. The second shipment to Ukraine of COVID-19 vaccine doses, as part of our donation of nearly 2.2 million doses, reaffirms that commitment. https://t.co/axPjpZY80y

The U.S. is proud to deliver the first batch of Johnson & Johnson vaccines to Yemen through #COVAX. These 151,200 vaccines are in addition to the more than $108 million we have provided for COVID-19 response to refugees, vulnerable migrants, IDPs, and host communities in Yemen. https://t.co/fzjdYJzga6

Today, the U.S. and nearly 100 other countries issued a Joint Statement on Afghanistan Evacuation Travel Assurances. Read more:
https://t.co/1Ku8i1MgIf

Providing safe and effective vaccines is critical to ending the COVID-19 pandemic. Today, the U.S. donated over 100,000 Pfizer vaccine doses to Sri Lanka, in addition to the 1.5 million Moderna doses donated in July, to keep more Sri Lankans safe and protected from the virus. https://t.co/1cQsCY8CRs

Through global vaccine donations, the United States is leading efforts to protect the world from the threat of COVID-19. We are proud to announce an additional delivery of 188,370 doses of the Pfizer vaccine to El Salvador in partnership with #COVAX. https://t.co/UeGVlxdtvj

The United States is committed to defeating the global COVID-19 pandemic alongside our international partners. We are proud to announce the delivery of 604,800 doses of the Johnson & Johnson vaccine to Algeria. https://t.co/MWxlSCG6Yd

The United States provided 3.7 million Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Pakistan, in addition to the 5.5 million Moderna vaccines delivered in July. Through our continued vaccination efforts and cooperation in public health, we can end the pandemic together. https://t.co/EHttZF3OUe

.@StateDeptSpox discusses the ongoing evacuation efforts in Afghanistan: Last night alone, we reached out to every American whom we believe may be in Afghanistan and attempting to leave. In many cases, we did this multiple times by phone, email, and text. https://t.co/ypO93dRlrf

.@StateDeptSpox gives remarks on yesterday’s news in Kabul: Yesterday was a dark day, yet our service members, alongside our diplomatic personnel, have been back at it today, guarding the facility in Kabul and facilitating the most noble of missions. https://t.co/VKLLBvqrA8
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/3LDL7cxBHb

Today at approximately 2:30 p.m. EDT, @StateDeptSpox Ned Price will host a press briefing from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/ugwfgCtGA0. https://t.co/R9sdagxUmM

As @POTUS has said, the U.S. is committed to bringing the same urgency to international vaccination efforts that we have demonstrated at home. We are proud to have delivered 151,200 COVID-19 vaccines to the Republic of Congo today, working closely with @gavi and @_AfricanUnion. https://t.co/eeHnkEgNnN

The United States continues to make great strides in its efforts to distribute safe and effective vaccines globally. Which is why we are happy to announce our donation of 1,182,870 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Angola with our partner @_AfricanUnion via @gavi #COVAX. https://t.co/jGfM1XbDcM

.@SecBlinken on Afghanistan: In all, more than two dozen countries on four continents are contributing to the effort to transit, temporarily house, or resettle those who we are evacuating. We are deeply grateful to those countries for their generous assistance. https://t.co/DlHdnhiRkO

.@POTUS directs the flag be flown at half-staff at @WhiteHouse and upon all public buildings and grounds, at all military posts and naval stations, and all U.S. diplomatic missions to honor the victims of the attack in Kabul, Afghanistan. https://t.co/RoGpTFXjL0
.@SecBlinken's statement on today's terrorist attack in Kabul: https://t.co/5XaFwzcR4q.

The United States is proud to work with #COVAX to deliver another vaccine donation to Honduras: 99,450 doses of the Pfizer vaccine. Vaccinations are an essential element in our shared battle to defeat COVID-19. https://t.co/M66oGBbhzj
In a 24-hour period from Tuesday-Wednesday, approximately 19,000 people were evacuated on 90 U.S. military and coalition flights.

.@SecBlinken: Let me begin with my profound appreciation for our diplomats and service members who are working around the clock at the airport in Kabul and at a growing number of transit sites...Since August 14, more than 82,300 people have been safely flown out of Kabul. https://t.co/DLqiG9TQAf
For those preferring to watch on YouTube, the updated link is: https://t.co/w6ASgNuaN1
Live now! Secretary of State @SecBlinken delivers remarks to the press on our efforts to bring Americans home and our support for the broader evacuation from Afghanistan. https://t.co/PR62gLLwHL
Secretary Blinken’s Remarks to the Press on Afghanistan will now begin at 2:30 p.m. EDT.

Today at approximately 12:15 p.m. EDT, Secretary of State @SecBlinken will host a press briefing on our efforts in Afghanistan, from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/YNOtPVjuaB. https://t.co/0aLq7ueJ32

Our global vaccine donation campaign continues. Today's delivery of 146,250 Pfizer doses to Guyana is another important step to help save lives and end the pandemic abroad and at home. https://t.co/q2BIyAVjb7

Today the United States delivered 1,750,000 Moderna vaccine doses to our friends and neighbors in Mexico. With a total of 5,820,000 vaccine doses facilitated to date, we are proud to help save lives and end the pandemic in Mexico and around the world. https://t.co/U9uysX7a3o

America's diversity is the strength of our nation, and it is reflected in @TeamUSA. No matter the race, religion, identity, or ability, we are stronger because of our differences. We honor what makes us unique, and we celebrate all of our athletes. #Tokyo2020 #SportsDiplomacy https://t.co/Nf3MoVpKaX

.@POTUS: I’ve been touched by the outpouring of support
that we’ve seen from communities and organizations across America. So many of these Afghans stood bravely by U.S. troops in Afghanistan. https://t.co/ZA0Ypp2oaL

.@POTUS on the airlift out of Afghanistan: “In order to keep a steady flow of planes taking off from Kabul and maximize our evacuation capacity, we have quickly stood up an unprecedented global effort and established a series of processing stations in third countries.” https://t.co/xdts0KwGIk

.@POTUS on efforts in Afghanistan: Any American who wants to get home will get home. We’ve also been evacuating the citizens of our @NATO Allies and our partners, including their diplomats, their embassy staff who remain in Afghanistan. Full remarks: https://t.co/ceyM3P5UE8. https://t.co/YygJ4NAOA3

.@StateDeptSPOX briefs the press on updates in Afghanistan: State Department employees are working around-the-clock to help evacuate American citizens. Our team made several thousand calls over the weekend and those efforts continue. https://t.co/iATjPO1Ic5
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/j3A6U9LVms

.@RamsteinAirBase in Germany is serving among the first stops for evacuees from Afghanistan. We are proud to work alongside our allies to support the relocation efforts for Americans and at-risk Afghans. https://t.co/ddtCaxOtUc

Today at approximately 3:30 p.m. EDT, @StateDeptSpox Ned Price will host a press briefing from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/ynsXl6QCDu. https://t.co/6c3pjyOYLX

The United States is committed to leading the global effort to stop COVID-19. Working closely with #COVAX, we will donate 188,370 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Togo to bring us one step closer to this goal. https://t.co/3478RZwp1M
More on our efforts to assist the people of Haiti can be found at: https://t.co/ASNYTygZ0m

On August 14, a magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck Haiti causing thousands of injuries and death. The United States is supporting efforts to assist and working with communities to rebuild lives. We stand with the people of Haiti in the aftermath of this tragedy. https://t.co/j5jEfotprO

With our partner @_AfricanUnion via @gavi #COVAX, we successfully donated 880,460 doses of the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine to Kenya. Sharing these COVID-19 vaccines serves as an example of the strength of our partnership and commitment to the people of Kenya against COVID-19. https://t.co/F4MoU6oVtR
Around the world, @statedept consular officers are working nonstop to support any American who may be present in Afghanistan and wishing to depart. https://t.co/voUOYBFlkQ

At Ramstein Air Base in Germany, @StateDept personnel are welcoming evacuees from Afghanistan and helping facilitate their onward travel. Coordinating with our interagency partners, we are focused on safely getting as many people out of Afghanistan as quickly as possible. https://t.co/eg4sL8Kmkt

The United States has mobilized an unprecedented, global effort through our diplomatic channels to evacuate Americans, personnel from partner nations, and the at-risk Afghans from Kabul. We are working around the clock to maximize evacuations. https://t.co/HJyTVm00Sr

The United States partnered with COVAX to ensure that 1,180,530 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine arrived in Côte d’Ivoire. These vaccines do not come with strings attached. They are a part of our commitment to save lives and end the pandemic. https://t.co/uZo8NzaNIj

With our friends @_AfricanUnion the U.S. is donating 81,900 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to Botswana. With variants spreading and people at risk everyday, via @gavi #COVAX we will continue to play our part in the fight against the pandemic. https://t.co/etJ8BVPHdk

As @POTUS has said, the United States is committed to bringing the same urgency to international vaccination efforts that we have demonstrated at home. We are proud to have delivered 489,060 COVID-19 vaccine doses to Rwanda working closely with @gavi and @_AfricanUnion. https://t.co/C66XeBlT9p

On the anniversary of the poisoning of Russian opposition figure Aleksey Navalny, @StateDeptSpox comments on the additional costs being imposed on the Russian Federation, and also discusses the sanctioning of Russian individuals in connection with Nord Stream 2. https://t.co/x8iaGoSmuT

.@StateDeptSpox on Afghanistan: Our diplomats–whether in Kabul, here in Washington, elsewhere–have been working around the clock, literally, to coordinate evacuations; line up partner countries to help; and lay the groundwork for a diplomatic approach moving forward. https://t.co/lZqJiGYikg
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State.  https://t.co/6vhtI8FQKC

Today at approximately 3 p.m. EDT, @StateDeptSpox Ned Price will host a press briefing from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/EnCjRxEEyL. https://t.co/vOGbGBjV0w

.@POTUS pledged to assist partners in the fight against COVID-19 and we are delivering. Today the American people provided the first tranche of over 500,000 safe and effective Pfizer doses headed to Kosovo. This is the largest single vaccine donation to Kosovo by any country. https://t.co/misuE6uRoz

The quickest path to ending the pandemic is through safe and effective vaccine distribution. We are proud to send 208,260 doses of the Pfizer vaccine to Jamaica today. The U.S. remains the world’s partner in ending COVID-19 and working toward a healthier future. https://t.co/icWc1xF1vB

In the battle to defeat COVID-19, we are pleased to announce that this week 52,650 doses of the Pfizer vaccine arrived in St. Lucia and 29,250 doses arrived in Grenada. Only by continuing to vaccinate the world can we move forward together. https://t.co/bxe51La9i0

.@StateDeptSpox: Our diplomats around the world are tirelessly engaging with their counterparts to ensure transit and passage for Americans, vulnerable Afghans and others. This is absolutely an all-hands-on-deck effort to ensure the safety of our personnel and citizens. https://t.co/z5GsawtJ8w

On #WorldHumanitarianDay, we thank all those who are working at home and abroad to alleviate suffering and assist those in need through humanitarian efforts. https://t.co/qZz1i0GNe1 https://t.co/IC1f76yl7I
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/pezTcZOzuY

“Afghan women and girls, as all Afghan people, deserve to live in safety, security, and dignity.” The U.S. and the international community remain committed to assisting with humanitarian aid and support​ to ensure their voices can be heard. Read more: https://t.co/BdmGr5ZMWo.

The United States is proud to donate the first 111,150 of the over 345,000 Pfizer vaccine doses Belize will receive, continuing our efforts to help the people of the Western Hemisphere and the world combat COVID-19. Vaccinations make us all safer. https://t.co/ZhxM6cu4Lr

.@DeputySecState Sherman: "The United States and the international community will be vigilant in monitoring how any future government in Afghanistan ensures the rights and freedoms that women and girls in that country have come to expect." https://t.co/9EC0I9ge1k

.@DeputySecState Sherman: "This has been an all-hands-on-deck effort to ensure the safety of our personnel and citizens, rally our allies and partners, and organize the evacuation of thousands of Afghans." https://t.co/iKfvhY5V73
Live now! @DeputySecState Wendy R. Sherman delivers remarks on Afghanistan, followed by a press briefing from @StateDeptSpox Ned Price. https://t.co/6GCrx5aLs4

At approximately 2:30 p.m. EDT, @DeputySecState Sherman will deliver remarks on Afghanistan, immediately followed by a press briefing by @StateDeptSpox Price, from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7, and https://t.co/nDbFoN8HW7. https://t.co/5XUOksVj8I
.@POTUS: American troops cannot and should not be fighting in a war and dying in a war that Afghan forces are not willing to fight for themselves. https://t.co/tcTYe2e1l1
.@POTUS: Today, the terrorist threat has metastasized well beyond Afghanistan. These threats warrant our attention and our resources. https://t.co/5M3ciVw66F

.@POTUS: I am deeply saddened by the facts we now face. But I do not regret my decision to end America’s warfighting in Afghanistan and maintain a laser-focus on our counterterrorism missions there and in other parts of the world. https://t.co/NqAwG4Agwe

.@POTUS: Over the coming days, we intend to transport out thousands of American citizens who have been living and working in Afghanistan. We’ll also continue to support the safe departure of the civilian personnel of our Allies who are still serving in Afghanistan. https://t.co/ZmDIzZG7yL

.@POTUS: We will continue to support the Afghan people. We will lead with our diplomacy, our international influence, and our humanitarian aid. We’ll continue to speak out for the basic rights of the Afghan people — of women and girls — just as we speak out all over the world. https://t.co/exbRYV3NrQ

.@POTUS: We went to Afghanistan almost 20 years ago with clear goals. It was never supposed to be creating a unified, centralized democracy. Our only vital national interest in Afghanistan remains today what it has always been: preventing a terrorist attack on American homeland. https://t.co/0WA5jpSdmK

.@StateDeptSpox provides an update on the situation in Afghanistan: "We remain committed to accelerating flights for SIVs and other vulnerable Afghans as quickly as possible. The safety and security of U.S. Government employees and U.S. citizens overseas is our top priority." https://t.co/x4qhx3Snt9
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/nuR6VcsEGM

Today at approximately 2:30 p.m. EDT, @StateDeptSpox Ned Price will host a press briefing from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7, and https://t.co/KDEeqS4l1k. https://t.co/pfnzuGB2Ph

In our shared fight against the pandemic, the United States delivered 400,000 doses of single-shot Johnson & Johnson vaccine to the Republic of Korea. The United States is committed to the US-ROK partnership.

.@StateDeptSpox on Haiti: "We offer our deepest condolences to all those who suffered the loss of loved ones or saw their homes or businesses destroyed. We are in close contact with the Haitian authorities to respond to the earthquake and any requests for assistance." https://t.co/0iRgpmZkvd

.@StateDeptSpox discusses the developing situation in Afghanistan, including our efforts to protect U.S. citizens abroad, relocate interested and qualified Afghan SIV applicants, and coordinate with our international partners. https://t.co/4Zbx7BqERi

Bold and ambitious collaborative action is necessary to combat the climate crisis. Voices around the world agree on the urgency of @COP26 to make a pivotal difference in ramping up our global climate efforts. See more: https://t.co/0ZDki6Qgfh. https://t.co/NptK1my0GA

The United States is working alongside our international partners to end the global COVID-19 pandemic. Today, we are proud to announce the delivery of 500,000 doses of the Pfizer vaccine to Iraq. https://t.co/IwqsYEegf7

.@ClimateEnvoy: To meet the challenge, at the Leaders’ Summit in April, the United States announced an ambitious target of reducing our emissions by 50-52 percent by 2030. We do all this knowing full well that no country and no continent alone can solve the climate crisis. https://t.co/ZmjRhcel0i
.@SecBlinken: Today, the science is unequivocal: We need to keep the Earth’s warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius to avoid catastrophe. https://t.co/dtHMNjYVER

The newest @IPCC_CH report finds we are already edging closer to a 1.5 degrees Celsius warmer world—and why it is essential that all countries ​increase their ambition and action during this critical decade. More: https://t.co/wJ5hrva8EU.

Here at the State Department, we offer youth networks such as @YALINetwork, @YLAINetwork and @YSEALI that help empower young people and enable them to play an important role in foreign affairs. Find an exchange program right for you at https://t.co/VXIEo3ac0F.

Listen to Coordinator for Global COVID-19 Response and Health Security Gayle Smith, as she discusses the latest updates on the United States' shipments of vaccine doses worldwide and our efforts to work with our partners and allies to overcome the pandemic.
.@POTUS: Vaccine donations from America are free. There are no demands, no conditions, no coercion attached. We’re doing this to save lives and to end this pandemic. https://t.co/f8GtbTzhxU

As part of our pledge to provide 25 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to @_AfricanUnion member states, the United States is proud to announce the delivery of 302,400 doses of the Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine to our friends in Somalia via @gavi, #COVAX, and the AU. https://t.co/YipRsRPf3S

We must have equitable access to COVID-19 vaccines if we want to build a safer and more secure world. The United States has taken one step closer to this goal by sending 305,370 doses of the Pfizer vaccine to our friends in Trinidad and Tobago. https://t.co/JIJp0Zlipt

.@StateDeptSpox: We call on Syria to help release Austin Tice and every U.S. citizen held hostage in Syria. @SecBlinken is personally dedicated to seeking the safe release of U.S. hostages and wrongful detainees. https://t.co/7Vhz6tYzS1

Today, on the one-month anniversary of the Cuban people's call for freedom, @StateDeptSpox reaffirmed the United States’ commitment to the Cuban people.  We will not look away from Cuban Government repression. https://t.co/6HJYIVKae1

.@SecBlinken: By investing in our strength at home, we can show the world that democracies can do hard things – and that we can do them without jailing dissidents, letting corruption run rampant, or violating fundamental freedoms. https://t.co/A4gR87Klnh

Since the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic, the United States has provided $4.6 million in assistance to Maldives. Our additional donation of 128,700 doses of the Pfizer vaccine will help keep more Maldivians safe and protected from the virus. https://t.co/ocXEr3TJfZ

.@SecBlinken: We became the United States of America because past generations of Americans reinvented, renewed, and reinvested in our core sources of strength. Now it’s our job to take up the baton and carry it forward for future generations. https://t.co/gn4Qy2kLKQ

.@SecBlinken: The creativity, ingenuity, and dynamism of the American private sector is unmatched. But there are some things that even the most vibrant private sector can’t do on its own. Public investment is still vital. https://t.co/xzWsJAvl7N

.@SecBlinken: By investing in our strength at home, we can show the world that democracies can do hard things and that we can do them without jailing dissidents, letting corruption run rampant, or violating fundamental freedoms. More: https://t.co/VGOIYnmFye.
.@SecBlinken: There’s nothing we can do that will enhance our global standing and influence more than what our domestic renewal can deliver. https://t.co/opiPFdOglo

.@SecBlinken: We’ll continue to call on our partners around the world to join us in condemning the [People's Republic of China] genocide and crimes against humanity in Xinjiang and in taking steps to prevent goods made with forced labor from entering our supply chains. https://t.co/GYeKNVjOJO

Over the last four years, the People's Republic of China has carried out a mass detention and political indoctrination campaign against Uyghurs, who are predominantly Muslim, and members of other ethnic and religious minority groups in Xinjiang. https://t.co/BE073xI0b5

Human rights experts agree. The PRC has been committing crimes against humanity in Xinjiang, China against the predominantly Muslim Uyghurs, and members of other ethnic and religious minority groups. The facts are clear: These human rights atrocities must end. https://t.co/4WXXoS5bSK

The United States continues to lead global efforts to save lives, with additional assistance for Burma and Thailand. We urge other countries and stakeholders to contribute to the humanitarian response in both nations. https://t.co/hkxQ88vMkD

.@POTUS: We know that COVID-19 in other countries stifles economic growth, disrupts supply chains, risks instability and weakness of governments.  And as we’ve seen in the United States, the key to growing economies is to vaccinate people. https://t.co/3rwt3WX2WF

Today, on #IndigenousPeoplesDay, we recognize the Indigenous communities who continue to address the climate crisis and work to protect our Earth. Read more on how we are partnering with Indigenous groups on bold climate action: https://t.co/sE4bZ9Drmw

The newest @IPCC_CH report finds we are already edging closer to a 1.5 degrees Celsius warmer world—and why it is essential that all countries ​increase their ambition and action during this critical decade. More: https://t.co/wJ5hrvrK3u.

.@POTUS: We need to attack this virus globally, not just at home. The virus knows no boundaries. https://t.co/I3PtsdMSnC

Bold and ambitious collaborative action is necessary to combat the climate crisis. Voices around the world agree on the urgency of @COP26 to make a pivotal difference in ramping up our global climate efforts. See more: https://t.co/0ZDki6yFnJ. https://t.co/twQimkag4o

.@POTUS: “Meeting the moment on climate change must begin with a recognition that every nation has a responsibility and every nation is at risk.” More: https://t.co/7Igaq1rnnq. https://t.co/dbY65SQWaF

No one can tackle the climate crisis alone. All countries, governments, industries, and citizens must work together to find climate solutions. Together, we can chart a path for a more secure, prosperous, and sustainable future for all. https://t.co/XHPoJc2XaU

.@POTUS on the climate crisis: We have to move. We have to move quickly to meet these challenges. The United States has now shipped over 110 million COVID-19 vaccination doses in an effort to get safe and effective vaccines to as many people around the world as fast as possible. Read more about our deliveries and see where they have gone here: https://t.co/kw2k3Esezi. https://t.co/1mju3pjGkP

The United States is committed to ending the COVID-19 pandemic and leading global response efforts. To date, we have donated 23M+ vaccine doses and over $158M in emergency health and humanitarian assistance to @ASEAN members. Learn more: https://t.co/QF6r685xsU. https://t.co/4EelCL5tIo

We will not stop until the COVID-19 pandemic has been defeated at home and abroad, and we will continue to work to build a safer and more secure world.

See our deliveries in action in our Flickr album here: https://t.co/84Nb0jKpQm.
